subject: Revert "[POLICY] legacy pdp health + new image versions"
commit_message:
Revert "[POLICY] legacy pdp health + new image versions"

This reverts commit 110657a0dcf682f7b5b0d533a36a36fe0df0cc84.

Reason for revert: it looks that this commit broke policy HC

Change-Id: Icb24fb01b9507fb44d5adcc62345713b8ebf8f1f
Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Opasiak <k.opasiak@samsung.com>
Issue-ID: POLICY-2471

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 1 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Orange 3: Chained-CI build started, max time: 14400 seconds

Patch Set 1:

FYI

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

Hmm I've no idea what happen but policy HC seems to work now.
@Morgan
Have we had some change in tests or sth?

I'm giving -2 as this one won't be needed if everything works fine

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Instead of just reverting, let's just submit a review that fixes the bug if there is one.

Patch Set 1:

As a side note, we have been checking the gating results daily since this review and they have all succeeded in the health check.

https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/onap_daily_pod4_master-515734774-04-17-2020_10-04/healthcheck/full/full/report.html

Patch Set 1:

I just tested healthcheck and I don't see a problem (as gating results seem ok too).   We can investigate further if you can provide us with the error details.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I just tested healthcheck and I don't see a problem (as gating results seem ok too).   We can investigate further if you can provide us with the error details.

Hi Jorge,
have you merged some changes to your tests recently?
Main reason for this patch was that policy HC was failing yesterday in almost all reviews (even on those that don't touch policy) and that's the reason for this revert. Interestingly today everything is back to normal (and that's why I gave myself -2 on this patch) and I don't really know what happen...

> Main reason for this patch was that policy HC was failing yesterday in almost all reviews (even on those that don't touch policy) and that's the reason for this revert. Interestingly today everything is back to normal (and that's why I gave myself -2 on this patch) and I don't really know what happen...

Not sure, I cannot pinpoint it to any particular code changes that we had on our side lately.   Have there been any changes perhaps that relates to the policy db mariadb galera side?   I'm wondering if that may have been triggering some inestabilities,but not sure.

> Not sure, I cannot pinpoint it to any particular code changes that we had on our side lately.   Have there been any changes perhaps that relates to the policy db mariadb galera side?   I'm wondering if that may have been triggering some inestabilities,but not sure.

Well it could be the case if we change the masterPassword for every deployment but masterPassword (which means that also all other passwords) are constant in our gating... so if it worked once it has to work also next 10 times..

And BTW. in the evening I've re triggered gating on your patch that I'm reverting here and the HC was failing on that patch:

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/105733

I've just retriggered it once again on that review and let's see what's the result today...

I'll keep this patch only for reference (but with -2) and if everything is OK during next week I'll just abandon it.

kubernetes/policy/charts/drools/resources/configmaps/feature-healthcheck.properties.
We'll keep an eye on it, to see if it was just a random occurrence, and if needed we could remove the change in that file.
